question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, showing that CPI increased by only 0.3% in March 2026 on a seasonally adjusted basis. Since 0.3% is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for a YES resolution, this market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty. The market is currently pricing 68% for YES when the outcome is definitively NO.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased 0.3%
Market closes April 10, 2026 - after data release
No possibility of data revision changing the outcome
risks: Extremely minimal risk of data revision affecting the result
Market mechanics may prevent immediate price adjustment
